author: javierabosch2
text: Creating a new Column inside a for loop, then trying to access raises an IndexError.

author: javierabosch2
text:
Linux Ubuntu 18.04  
Modin version 0.8.1.1  
Python Version 3.7.9

When Creating a new column inside a for loop, trying to access that column raises an IndexError: List index out of range.

Below I am assigning a new column to each item in a list. sub\_topics is a column containing a list of 5 tags.

```
for i in range(5):
    data[f'st_tag_{i}'] = data['sub_topics'].map(lambda x: x[i])
    print(data[f'st_tag_{i}'].head(2)) # Error caused here.
```

author: javierabosch2
text:
When accessing and working on the modin DataFrame instead of Series.map, it worked. So putting an additional [] around the column accessor did the trick. I am not sure if the modin team would want to address this.

Working version:

for i in range(5):  
data[f'st\_tag\_{i}'] = data[['sub\_topics']].apply(lambda x: x[i]) # Fixed Error  
print(data[f'st\_tag\_{i}'].head(2)) # Can access column now.
